CONDITIONAL. Foundry Yard clears the hard financial screens on T12 NOI: 60.0% LTV, 1.29x amortizing DSCR, and 9.41% debt yield, with no threshold dealbreaker. The rent roll also supports 94.17% physical occupancy and only modest 3.0% loss-to-lease. The condition is the revenue-quality risk: the OM says approximately 60% of residents work for Carolina Logistics, and current rent roll billing is $305k above T12 EGI, so the team needs employer-concentration and collections reconciliation before treating this as a clean core-plus proceed.

CONDITIONAL. The rent roll itself is mostly clean for a core-plus screen: 120 units, 94.17% physical occupancy, modest loss-to-lease, and going-in amortizing DSCR of about 1.25x using the scenario-adjusted 5.80% rate at 65% LTV. The condition is the planted T12 insurance issue: the $41K annual insurance line is materially below multifamily benchmark support for Dallas/North Texas, and normalizing it compresses NOI and DSCR into the conditional band. No rent-roll dealbreaker found.

FAIL. Harborview is a no-go as structured: the rent roll supports 62.0% physical occupancy, and the deal file explicitly says the asset is financed with permanent debt and no bridge/lease-up capital. That directly hits the threshold dealbreaker for occupancy below 70% without a committed bridge structure or funded lease-up plan, and the going-in amortizing DSCR is only 0.10x.

perDealbreaker.matchedText: FAIL. Harborview is a no-go as structured: the rent roll supports 62.0% physical occupancy, and the deal file explicitly says the asset is financed with permanent debt and no bridge/lease-up capital. That directly hits the threshold dealbreaker for occupancy below 70% without a committed bridge structure or funded lease-up plan, and the going-in amortizing DSCR is only 0.10x.

perFlag.matchedText: Phase I ESA is missing per the deal context and no Phase I document was found in the deal documents.

CONDITIONAL. Cedar Industrial Lofts is not a rent-roll dealbreaker: 89.84% occupancy is just below the 90% acceptable threshold but well above distress levels, LTV is clean at 70%, and going-in amortizing DSCR is 1.15x, inside the conditional band rather than fail territory. The value-add thesis is plausible because occupied rents are 6.58% below stated market rent, but the deal needs revenue reconciliation before proceeding because the current rent-roll run-rate is materially higher than T12 EGI.

CONDITIONAL. Crestline Park has a real but moderate rent-roll upside story: 91.0% physical occupancy, $204,480 of annual occupied-unit loss-to-lease, and another $288,960 of vacant-unit market rent potential. The issue is not the rent roll by itself; it is the leverage placed on it. At 82% LTV, going-in DSCR is only 0.85x on amortizing debt and 0.99x interest-only, while debt yield is 6.36%. Because this is a value-add deal with a stated pro forma NOI that would support coverage if achieved, this is not a FAIL, but proceeds should be re-cut or conditioned on verified collections, lease-up, and lower leverage.
